published_prior_art.cite: YuShiTchetgen2025 defines the fortified space but does not give the general weighted-array count; this is a direct finite-dimensional expansion of that definition.
YuShiTchetgen2025, Assumption 5 and Theorem 2.1 assume/use fortified completeness but do not state a finite-support supply-versus-demand impossibility region.
No located paper states this monotonicity, but it is an immediate arithmetic corollary of the proposed dimension formula.
YuShiTchetgen2025, Remark 2.3, prints a 12-by-15 matrix; the proposed 12-by-16 correction follows from adding all three ternary complement directions.

AllmanMatiasRhodes2009 supplies a generic-identifiability algebraic template and Gu2025BlessingDependence warns that constrained latent models can defeat counts, but neither treats this fortified-completeness completion family.

novelty_flags.label: Proposal-level comparator commitments
novelty_flags.one_line: The prose repeatedly frames YuShiTchetgen2025 as the comparator that is strictly tightened, but the required six-row comparator-promise table is absent, so the comparator-to-statement commitments are not auditable.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2 (thm:universal-rank-barrier)
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem assumes full-cell support but concludes that no law in \(\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) can satisfy Assumption 5, although that component definition does not include full-cell support; restrict the conclusion to its full-cell subcomponent.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 (thm:universal-rank-barrier)
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The barrier concerns only injectivity of YuShiTchetgen2025 Assumption 5's fortified outcome-completeness pairing, not ATE nonidentification or alternative bridge routes.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Conditional on full-cell support and the selected-component proxy restrictions, negative slack rules out that fortified outcome-bridge completeness premise only.

source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 9--12, equation (2.3), Assumption 5, Remark 2.3, and Theorem 2.1
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper defines \(H_\gamma\) by simultaneous conditional-mean restrictions, defines fortified completeness as injectivity against \(H_\gamma+L^2(Z_{-\nu^*},X)\), prints a 12-by-15 categorical illustration, and identifies the ATE under Assumptions 3--5 plus consistency.
The article exists at the cited venue/year and states nonparametric identification with two independent proxies satisfying a rank condition.

source_verification_receipts.version: arXiv:2303.10134v4, 1 October 2023; corroborating version of the cited SPL article
source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 1--5, abstract, Theorems 2.1--2.2 discussion, and Proposition 2.3
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper states that ATE/ATT can be identified as linear functionals without uniqueness of a bridge and develops inference without bridge uniqueness; the primary journal PDF was not needed for a formula-mismatch finding.

source_verification_receipts.version: arXiv:0809.5032 preprint corresponding to Annals of Statistics 37(6A), 3099--3132
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper establishes generic identifiability in latent-structure models through algebraic/Kruskal-rank arguments, a relevant generic-minor methodology rather than a fortified-proxy result.

The paper studies discrete latent graphical models and shows that structural latent-dependence restrictions determine generic/strict identifiability, so naive dimension counting alone is insufficient in constrained latent families.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119(547), 1778--1793
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article reduces discrete causal bounding problems to polynomial programs and gives automated sharp bounds; it does not provide a fortified-completeness dimension count.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the missing six-row comparator-promise table and make Theorem 2 explicitly state its strict necessary-condition comparison to YuShiTchetgen2025 Assumption 5, with the cited page/remark anchors.

Repair Theorem 2 so every conclusion is quantified over \(P\in\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) satisfying full-cell support, then add the \(K=2,m=3,u=2\) 12-by-16 calculation as a corollary of that corrected theorem.

Develop the compatible-minor handle into a generic-attainment theorem or a concrete obstruction family for an infinite parameter regime; that would turn the necessary arithmetic frontier into an exact feasibility frontier rather than a count-only correction.

recommended_next_step: Revise the comparator documentation and full-support quantifier in Theorem 2, then advance the retained field-tier kernel to Stage 0.
